I've spent 20 actions and still haven't completed the level. I need to completely rethink this. Let me check if the agent moved after clicking on the top of the blue block.
Actually, let me step back and think about this problem from scratch. I've been assuming the agent moves when I click on blue blocks, but maybe I'm wrong about the game mechanics entirely.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check if agent moved after clicking top of blue block
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

print("Agent position after clicking top of blue block:")
for label, seg in [("BEFORE", before), ("AFTER", after)]:
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 16:
            b = n['boundary']
            rows = [p[0] for p in b]
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            print(f"  {label}: bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")

# Let me trace through ALL transitions to understand what changed
print("\n\n=== FULL HISTORY TRACE ===")
for i, entry in enumerate(history):
    seg = entry.frame.segmentation
    agent_bbox = None
    pink_px = None
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 16:
            b = n['boundary']
            rows = [p[0] for p in b]
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            agent_bbox = f"[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]"
        if n['color'] == 'P':
            pink_px = n['pixels']
    print(f"  Action {i+1}: {entry.action} -> agent={agent_bbox}, pink={pink_px}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
